Author: deempak
Content:
Hello 
I have a few questions 
Is the precheck that happens on the platform same the one we can run locally from the GitHub? 
And if i upload gds now can i change it before the 15th . 
What about the chip id does the platform add it  later ?

Author: rebelmike
Content:
Yes you can make revisions. Though possibly not after you hit submit for manufacture. It runs the precheck without you doing that though, and yes I believe the precheck is the same as the one in GitHub.
The platform populates the id automatically

Author: deempak
Content: Or if there is any doc for this information can anyone link that for me please

Author: deempak
Content: Where do i get the Project Id it asks while creating a project

Author: rebelmike
Content: The four character one? You just choose your own id

Author: mole99
Content: FYI, you can still submit a new revision for manufacturing until the tapeout deadline.

Author: rebelmike
Content: Cool, I was slightly scared of that button 😆

Author: mole99
Content:
Haha, no need to be scared 😄 
Submit early, submit often!

Author: namibj
Content: I assume it works essentially like the equivalent on TinyTapeout does?

Author: essen__
Content: ~~Oh the subject of project ID, how is that macro generated ? Is it the "dummy" filled by the wafer space backend ?~~ Answered my own question 😅 It is done by the `GenerateID` step, as part of the precheck.

Author: mithro_
Content: @Deepak - The platform is suppose to give you the command line it ran, so you can replicate a failing run locally.
